items.title: Liberty jobs secure for now, but what happens when the money runs out?

children.content: Three weeks of wages have been guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers, but with no buyer locked in, questions remain about what happens next.

articles.title: Three weeks' pay guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers

children.content: A short-term rescue package announced by the Tasmanian and federal governments will guarantee the wages of workers at the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter.

image.altText: A large manufacturing building.

items.title: Embattled salmon industry launches advertising blitz amid calls to shut down

items.title: How to manage grief and trauma during painful anniversaries

children.content: Next week marks 30 years since the shooting at Port Arthur where 35 people lost their lives. How can you support loved ones when anniversaries bring back painful memories?

image.altText: Port Arthur memorial pool

items.title: Police officer given 'golden pass' on rape allegations, victim says

children.content: When Tasmania Police misidentified Laura as a family violence perpetrator, she had no idea she'd have to fight for them to hold her abuser — a serving officer — to account. Now, she's fighting for change.

items.title: Carpooling an 'effective solution' to fuel prices, so why aren't more doing it?

children.content: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

image.altText: Woman leaning on car.

children.content: "Worrisome" construction delays plague the US Navy's submarine-building program, which Australia is relying on for its naval fleet.

items.title: Sheraz risked his life translating in Afghanistan. His family paid the price

children.content: Sheraz worked with Australian and US troops in Afghanistan, as a result his family were targeted by the Taliban. This Anzac Day he's being heroed.

items.title: Russell Brand admits to sex with 16yo when he was 30

children.content: British actor and comedian Russell Brand, who is facing multiple sexual assault charges, has admitted to having sex with a 16-year-old when he was 30.

items.title: War on hoons

children.content: You might have heard of hooning – in which people — usually young men — drift, race, and do burnouts on public roads.
It happens across the country and has been around for decades. But, it’s getting bigger, louder and more visible.
So are police crackdowns. Fines, car seizures and jail terms are all on the cards for people caught hooning.
Despite the laws and serious dangers, people still do it. But, why?

image.altText: Blue backgroun with white text. Text reads Inside Australia's illegal drift racing world. ABC News Loop logo

items.title: Authors leave publisher after kids book scrapped for illustrator's comments

children.content: Writers including Evelyn Araluen and Randa Abdel-Fattah have left University of Queensland Press after the publisher axed publication of an Indigenous kids book over its illustrator's past "anti-Semitic" comments.

items.title: 'We're not ready to leave': Inside the messy dynamic of a no-grounds eviction

items.title: No more green bin pick-ups for some residents amid fuel uncertainty

items.title: Farmers say an easy solution to the mouse plague has been rejected
